title: Pericopsis elata
taxonomy: Pericopsis elata is a species of tree that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is classified in the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Pericopsis. Pericopsis elata is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is also known as the Yellow Meranti. The tree grows up to 50 meters tall and has a broad, flat crown. Pericopsis elata is a valuable timber tree, prized for its durable and versatile wood.
uses: Pericopsis elata is a tropical tree species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and roots of the tree are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The extracts from the plant are also believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some African cultures, Pericopsis elata is used to treat diarrhea, stomach ulcers, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits of Pericopsis elata for human health.
benefits: Pericopsis elata is a tree species native to Southeast Asia, and it has been studied for its potential health benefits. Research suggests that the tree's wood and bark contain various compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The extracts from Pericopsis elata may have antioxidant and antifungal effects, which could contribute to its potential medicinal uses. More research is required to fully understand the health benefits associated with this tree species.

intro: Pericopsis elata, also known as the Palla or Yellow Palla, is a slow-growing, deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The bark and leaves of Pericopsis elata have been used in traditional medicine for their reported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pericopsis elata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pericopsis elata is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the bark and leaves being dried and used in various herbal formulas to treat a range of health conditions.
